title: Saturday Threshold Confidence Check
objective: Validate threshold readiness with controlled over-under work instead of a maximal FTP test.
why_now: Thursday readiness said rest: tired, poor sleep, higher resting HR, and lower HRV. Moving the proof point to Saturday protects the quality work instead of forcing it tired.

source_context.week_objective: Restart rhythm and validate threshold readiness without catch-up fatigue.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. This is not a max FTP test. It is a controlled threshold-confidence check with RPE cap 8 and clean execution as the pass/fail. Do not start if Friday night or Saturday morning recovery is poor.

warmup.notes: Build gradually. Include 2 x 30s high-cadence openers if legs feel good.

nutrition.pre: Carb-forward meal 2-4h before with about 1-2g carbs/kg. Add 20-40g simple carbs 30-60 min before if starting hungry.
nutrition.during: Water is acceptable if well-fed. Use 20-40g carbs if the ride starts hungry or drifts over 60 minutes.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus 60-90g carbs because this is the week's key bike stimulus.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: optional caffeine only if tolerated and not late day, about 1-3 mg/kg 30-60 min before. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Carbs plus protein, fluids to thirst, and 5-10 min easy spin/walk if legs tighten.
recovery.evening: No extra hard training. Keep dinner digestible and protect sleep.
recovery.next_morning_check: If this lands at RPE 7-8 or lower with no fade, the next threshold progression can move up. If it costs more, hold the targets.

skill_focus.action: Rebuild power over 5-8 pedal strokes after corners or interruptions.
skill_focus.why: This tests usable outdoor threshold control instead of trainer-perfect numbers.

skill_focus.action: Keep cadence steady and avoid torque spikes late in each over.
skill_focus.why: Smooth torque protects the left calf and makes higher FTP work repeatable.

mental_focus.why: A controlled threshold file tells us more about your next training step than forcing a hard day after poor sleep.
mental_focus.reset: If power, cadence, or breathing gets ragged, ride 90 seconds at the low end of target; if it still feels forced, finish the current rep and skip the next over.

pre_interval_cues: Start only if Friday is low stress and Saturday morning sleep, HRV/resting HR, and legs are back to normal.
This is a confidence check, not a maximal FTP test.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Finish exactly as written. A clean pass is the signal to progress next week, not to improvise today.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Convert to 3 x 8 minutes at 88-92% or ride endurance only.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Both sets controlled, no desperation, no cadence collapse, and RPE no higher than 8.
